text_en: Bearing in mind that the prevention and elimination of corruption is a responsibility of all States and that States must cooperate with each other, with the support and participation of individuals and groups outside the public sector, such as civil society, non-governmental organizations and local associations, if they are to be effective in this regard,

text_en: Concerned about the difficulties, including practices, encountered by requested and requesting States in asset recovery, taking into account the particular importance of asset recovery for sustainable development and stability, and noting that it is difficult to provide adequate financial resources for the recovery of stolen assets.

items_en: Reiterating its concern at the seriousness of the problems and threats posed by corruption to the stability and security of societies, undermining democratic institutions and values as well as moral values and justice and jeopardizing sustainable development and the rule of law, in particular when an inadequate national and international response leads to impunity,
1. Takes note of the report of the Secretary-General; 3
2. Condemns corruption in all its forms, including the payment of bribes, as well as the laundering of the proceeds of corruption and other forms of economic crime;

3. Expresses its concern at the extent of corruption at all levels, including the volume of stolen assets and proceeds of corruption, and in this regard reiterates its commitment to preventing and combating corruption at all levels, in accordance with the United Nations Convention against Corruption 1;

4. Welcomes the fact that a considerable number of Member States have already ratified or acceded to the Convention, and in this regard urges all concerned Member States and regional economic integration organizations that have not yet done so to consider ratifying or acceding to the Convention as a matter of priority, within their competence, and calls upon all States parties to implement it fully as soon as possible;

5. Notes with appreciation the work done under the Mechanism for the Review of the Implementation of the United Nations Convention against Corruption and the Implementation Review Group, and urges Member States to continue to support them and to do their utmost to provide detailed information and to meet the deadlines set out in the guidelines for the conduct of country reviews by governmental experts and the secretariat; 4

6. Also notes with appreciation the work of the Open-ended Intergovernmental Working Groups on Asset Recovery and on the Prevention of Corruption, as well as the open dialogue with international organizations, welcomes the organization of open-ended intergovernmental expert group meetings on international cooperation, and invites States parties to the Convention to support the work of those bodies, including those of the Implementation Review Group, on technical assistance, as well as the continuation of the work of the above-mentioned Groups;

7. Reiterates the commitment of all States parties to the Convention to adopt effective measures at the national level and to cooperate at the international level in order to give full effect to chapter V of the Convention and to contribute effectively to the recovery of the proceeds of corruption;
8. Urges Member States to combat and suppress corruption in all its forms and the laundering of the proceeds of corruption, to prevent the

Encourage ÃĐgalement les Ãtats Membres Ã  lutter contre la corruption sous  toutes  ses  formes  en  accroissant  la  transparence,  l'intÃĐgritÃĐ,  le  sens  des responsabilitÃĐs et l'efficacitÃĐ au sein des secteurs public et privÃĐ, et considÃĻre Ã  cet ÃĐgard  qu'il  est  essentiel  d'engager  des  poursuites  contre  les  fonctionnaires corrompus et ceux qui les corrompent de faÃ§on Ã  lutter contre l'impunitÃĐ, et de
items_en: 9. Urges States parties to the Convention that have not yet done so to designate a central authority for international cooperation as provided for in the Convention and, where appropriate, focal points for asset recovery, and encourages them to consider as soon as possible requests for assistance from those bodies;

10. Encourages States parties to the Convention to use informal channels of communication, in particular before making a request for formal mutual legal assistance, and to promote such channels, including by designating officials or agencies, as appropriate, with technical expertise in the area of international cooperation in the recovery of assets to assist their counterparts in meeting the requirements for official legal assistance;
11. Calls upon States parties to the Convention to remove obstacles to asset recovery, including by simplifying their judicial procedures and preventing any diversion of assets;

12. Welcomes the outcome of the fourth session of the Conference of the States Parties to the United Nations Convention against Corruption, held in Marrakech, Morocco, from 24 to 28 October 2011, and invites States parties to the Convention to implement fully the resolutions of Conference 6;

13. Calls upon States parties to the Convention to provide each other with all possible assistance and cooperation in order to identify and recover stolen assets and proceeds of corruption and to examine closely and expeditiously the execution of requests for international judicial assistance, in accordance with the Convention, and to afford each other all possible assistance and cooperation in the extradition of persons accused of predicate offences, in accordance with their obligations under the Convention;

14. Urges States parties to the Convention to ensure that international cooperation procedures permit the seizure and detention of assets for the period necessary for their full preservation during the course of proceedings in another State, and to encourage or expand cooperation in the enforcement of foreign judgements, including through awareness-raising activities for judicial authorities, in accordance with the provisions of the Convention;

15. Encourages Member States, as appropriate and in accordance with their domestic legal systems, to consider assisting each other in the conduct of civil and administrative investigations and proceedings related to corruption cases;

16. Also encourages Member States to combat corruption in all its forms by increasing transparency, integrity, accountability and efficiency in the public and private sectors, and in this regard considers it essential to prosecute corrupt and corrupt officials in order to combat impunity, and encourages Member States to take appropriate measures to combat corruption in all its forms.

items_en: 17. Stresses the need for transparency in financial institutions, invites Member States to focus on identifying and tracking financial flows related to corruption, freezing or seizing assets derived from corruption for return, in accordance with the Convention, and encourages the strengthening of human and institutional capacities in this regard;

18. Calls upon States parties to the Convention to consider promptly requests for mutual legal assistance relating to the identification, tracing, freezing or recovery of proceeds of corruption and to respond effectively to requests for the exchange of information concerning proceeds of crime, property, equipment or other instruments referred to in article 31 of the Convention located in the territory of the requested State party, in accordance with the provisions of the Convention, including article 40 thereof;

19. Welcomes the efforts of Member States that have adopted laws and other practical measures to combat corruption in all its forms, and in this regard encourages Member States that have not yet done so to adopt such laws and to implement effective measures at the national level, as provided for in the Convention;

20. Reaffirms the need for Member States to take measures to prevent the transfer abroad and the laundering of assets derived from corruption, inter alia, to prevent the use of financial institutions in the country of origin and the country of destination to transfer or receive funds of illicit origin, and to recover such assets and return them to the requesting State, in accordance with the Convention;

21. Urges all Member States to respect the principles of good governance of public affairs and property, fairness, accountability and equality before the law, as well as the need to safeguard integrity and foster a culture of transparency, accountability and non-corruption, in accordance with the Convention;

22. Encourages enhanced international cooperation, inter alia, within the United Nations system, to support efforts at the national, subregional and regional levels to prevent and combat corruption and the transfer and laundering of the proceeds of corruption, in accordance with the principles of the Convention, and in this regard calls upon anti-corruption agencies, law enforcement agencies and financial intelligence agencies to work closely together;

23. Requests the Secretary-General to continue to provide the United Nations Office on Drugs and Crime with the necessary resources to enable it to work effectively for the implementation of the Convention and to fulfil the functions of the secretariat of the Conference of the States Parties to the Convention entrusted to it, and also requests the Secretary-General to ensure that the Mechanism for the Review of the Implementation of the Convention is adequately resourced, in accordance with the resolution adopted by the Conference of the States Parties; 7

items_fr: 24. 25. Exhorte la  communautÃĐ  internationale  Ã   fournir,  entre  autres,  une assistance technique Ã  l'appui de l'action menÃĐe au niveau national pour renforcer les  ressources  humaines  et  institutionnelles  afin  de  prÃĐvenir  et  de  combattre  la corruption et le transfert du produit de la corruption et de faciliter le recouvrement des avoirs et la restitution de ce produit conformÃĐment Ã  la Convention, et Ã  appuyer les  initiatives  nationales  visant  Ã   formuler  des  stratÃĐgies  pour  systÃĐmatiser  et promouvoir  la  lutte  contre  la  corruption,  la  transparence  et  l'intÃĐgritÃĐ  dans  les secteurs tant public que privÃĐ ;

26. 27. Encourage les Ãtats Membres Ã  ÃĐchanger et Ã  partager, y compris dans le cadre  des  organisations  rÃĐgionales  et  internationales, selon qu'il conviendra, les informations sur les enseignements tirÃĐs de l'expÃĐrience et les bonnes pratiques, ainsi que les informations liÃĐes aux activitÃĐs et initiatives d'assistance technique dans l'optique de renforcer l'action menÃĐe Ã  l'ÃĐchelle internationale pour prÃĐvenir et combattre la corruption ;

28. Prend note de l'Initiative pour le recouvrement des avoirs volÃĐs qu'ont lancÃĐe l'Office et la Banque mondiale et de la coopÃĐration instaurÃĐe avec d'autres partenaires concernÃĐs, y compris le Centre international pour le recouvrement des avoirs, et encourage la coordination entre les initiatives existantes ;

29. Se fÃĐlicite de la crÃĐation de l'AcadÃĐmie internationale de lutte contre la corruption, centre d'excellence consacrÃĐ Ã  l'enseignement, Ã  la formation et Ã  la recherche universitaire dans le domaine de la lutte contre la corruption, y compris le recouvrement des avoirs, et compte qu'elle dÃĐploiera des efforts soutenus Ã  cet ÃĐgard pour promouvoir les buts et l'application de la Convention ;

30. Prend note de la dÃĐcision prise par la ConfÃĐrence des Ãtats parties Ã  la Convention d'accepter la proposition du Gouvernement de la FÃĐdÃĐration de Russie d'accueillir sa sixiÃĻme session en 2015 8 , et remercie Ã  nouveau le Gouvernement panamÃĐen d'avoir proposÃĐ d'accueillir la cinquiÃĻme session de la ConfÃĐrence des Ãtats parties en 2013 ;
31. Prie le  SecrÃĐtaire  gÃĐnÃĐral,  eu  ÃĐgard  Ã   l'obligation  qui  lui  est  faite d'ÃĐtablir  des  rapports,  d'inclure  dans  le  rapport  qu'il  lui  prÃĐsentera  Ã   sa
items_en: 24. Reiterates its call upon the private sector, both at the national and international levels, including small and large enterprises and transnational corporations, to remain fully engaged in the fight against corruption, notes in this regard the role that the Global Compact can play in combating corruption and promoting transparency, and stresses the need for all stakeholders, including within the United Nations system, where appropriate, to continue to promote corporate accountability and accountability;

25. Urges the international community to provide, inter alia, technical assistance in support of national efforts to strengthen human and institutional resources to prevent and combat corruption and the transfer of proceeds of corruption and to facilitate asset recovery and return in accordance with the Convention, and to support national initiatives to formulate strategies to systematize and promote the fight against corruption, transparency and integrity in both the public and the private sectors;

26. Urges States parties and signatories to the Convention to strengthen the capacity of legislators, law enforcement officials, judges and prosecutors to address asset recovery issues, including in the areas of mutual legal assistance, confiscation, criminal confiscation and, where appropriate, dispossession without conviction, in accordance with their domestic law and the Convention, and in civil proceedings, and to accord the utmost importance to the provision of technical assistance in these areas, upon request;

27. Encourages Member States to exchange and share, including through regional and international organizations, as appropriate, information on lessons learned and good practices, as well as information related to technical assistance activities and initiatives with a view to strengthening international efforts to prevent and combat corruption;

28. Takes note of the Stolen Asset Recovery Initiative of the United Nations Office at Geneva and the World Bank and the cooperation with other relevant partners, including the International Centre for Asset Recovery, and encourages coordination among existing initiatives;

29. Welcomes the establishment of the International Anti-Corruption Academy as a centre of excellence for teaching, training and academic research in the field of anti-corruption, including asset recovery, and looks forward to continued efforts in this regard to promote the purposes and implementation of the Convention;

30. Takes note of the decision taken by the Conference of the States Parties to the Convention to accept the proposal of the Government of the Russian Federation to host its sixth session in 2015, 8 , and reiterates its appreciation to the Government of Panama for its offer to host the fifth session of the Conference of the States Parties in 2013;
31. Requests the Secretary-General, in view of his obligation to prepare reports, to include in his report to the General Assembly at its

text_en: sixty-eighth session, under the agenda item entitled "Crime prevention and criminal justice", a section entitled "Preventing and combating corruption and the transfer of proceeds of corruption, facilitating asset recovery and returning such assets to their legitimate owners, in particular to the countries of origin, in accordance with the United Nations Convention against Corruption", and also requests it to transmit to the General Assembly the report of the Conference of the States Parties to the Convention on its fifth session.
